retry_reason: The refcount decrement always succeeds; retrying is meaningless and would cause a double-put risk.
contract_summary: Put wrapper: decrements the reference count of the request_queue. If the refcount drops to zero, the queue is freed (via blk_free_queue). After calling this function, the caller must not access the queue object as it may be deallocated. The caller is expected to have previously acquired a reference (e.g., via blk_get_queue) that this put releases.

judge.reason: The sole purpose is to decrement the refcount of the request_queue and free it upon reaching zero. No other business logic is present.
